articles.title: HOUSE PRICE ANALYSIS BY COUNTRY: Scotland remains most affordable
articles.summary:
In her latest review of property prices by UK region, Kate Faulkner gives a more detailed analysis of the market in Scotland where it is currently cheapest.

articles.title: Government restates opposition to rent controls in clear split with Greens
articles.summary:
Greens and Labour clash over rent policy, with Housing Minister Matthew Pennycook claiming caps would make life more difficult for renters.

articles.title: Letting agents plan to raise fees ahead of Renters’ Rights Act
articles.summary:
Many agents will increase the fees they charge landlords, but risk losing them as clients, according to lettings tech firm Goodlord.

articles.title: Landlords ‘set to pull more than 200,000 rental properties this year’
articles.summary:
An estimated 220,000 homes will be withdrawn, with 65,000 due to the Renters' Rights Act, according to new research.

articles.title: Investors seek share of ‘£30bn’ leasehold reform losses
articles.summary:

articles.title: SNP ‘first refusal’ housing proposal comes under fire
articles.summary:
A proposal by Scottish First Minister, SNP leader John Swinney, to give private renters ‘first refusal’ to buy their homes, has come under fire from a property policy expert.

articles.title: England faces historic housing shortfall – households set to surge 17% by 2040
articles.summary:

articles.title: Northern Ireland’s top emerging investment hotspots

articles.title: Fleet Mortgages launches new BTL 2-year tracker
articles.summary:
Fleet Mortgages, the buy-to-let specialist lender, has today (10th April 2026) announced the launch of three new 75% LTV two-year tracker products available within its three buy-to-let lending ranges: Standard, Limited Company and HMO/Multi-Unit Freehold Block (MUFB).
The post Fleet Mortgages launches new BTL 2-year tracker appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: Yields up but war dents confidence, says Fleet
articles.summary:
Rental yields across England and Wales increased across every region annually in Q1 2026, offering a positive start to the year for landlords, according to Fleet Mortgages’ latest Quarterly Rental Barometer.

articles.title: New data shows Scotland’s appeal to Gulf buyers
